name: Beef Chili with Siete Taco Seasoning
ingredients:
2 2/3 lb ground beef (85/15)
1 white onion, finely diced
1 red bell pepper, finely diced
1 tsp garlic powder
1/2 tsp onion powder
1 tsp ground cumin
1 tbsp cumin seeds
1 tsp smoked paprika
1 tsp mild chili powder
1/4 tsp cinnamon
1/4 tsp cayenne pepper
2 tbsp tomato paste
28 oz no-salt-added crushed tomatoes
1 cup no-salt-added beef broth
1/2 cup no-salt-added kidney or black beans, drained & rinsed
1 packet Siete Mild Taco Seasoning (1.31 oz)
1/2 cup water
1 tbsp apple cider vinegar
1 tbsp olive oil
directions:
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add 1 tbsp cumin seeds and toast for 60–90 seconds, stirring constantly, until fragrant and just starting to darken. Watch carefully — they burn quickly.
Add the diced white onion and red bell pepper to the pot and cook until softened, about 5–6 minutes.
Add 2 2/3 lb ground beef and break it up well. Cook until fully browned with no pink remaining, about 7–8 minutes. With 2.7 lbs you may need to brown in two batches to avoid steaming. Drain excess fat if desired, leaving a little for flavor.
Sprinkle in 1 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp mild chili powder, 1/4 tsp cinnamon, and 1/4 tsp cayenne pepper. Add the Siete Mild Taco Seasoning packet and 1/2 cup water. Stir well to coat the meat and cook for 2–3 minutes, letting everything bloom and absorb into the beef.
Stir in 2 tbsp tomato paste and cook for 1–2 minutes, letting it caramelize slightly into the beef mixture for deeper flavor.
Pour in 28 oz crushed tomatoes and 1 cup beef broth. Stir in 1/2 cup kidney or black beans. Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low.
Simmer uncovered on low for at least 30 minutes, stirring occasionally. For best results, go the full hour to let it thicken and the flavors meld deeply.
Remove from heat and stir in 1 tbsp apple cider vinegar. This brightens the whole pot and balances the richness of the beef.
For younger babies, use an immersion blender to partially or fully puree to an age-appropriate texture. Toddlers 12+ months can usually handle it as-is with the meat well broken down.
notes:
Toast the cumin seeds until fragrant but watch them — they burn fast.
The cinnamon is subtle but important — don't skip it and don't increase it.
Apple cider vinegar goes in at the very end, off heat — this preserves its brightness.
The 1/4 tsp cayenne gives gentle background warmth across nearly 3 lbs of beef. Bump to 1/2 tsp for more adult heat.
Freezes well — portion into ice cube trays for easy baby servings.
Adults can finish bowls with extra hot sauce, red pepper flakes, or shredded cheese.
